info.name: Execute a new SQL query asynchronously

items.docs: Executes a SQL query asynchronously. You can check the status of a SQL query execution with the [Analysis:getAnalysisQueries API](#/Analysis/getAnalysisQueries).

info.name: Retrieve the execution status of a SQL query

params.name: query_id
params.description: SQL query ID.

items.docs: Retrieve the execution status of a SQL query.

Retrieves the available schemas and their column information within the database. You can use this information as a reference when converting data types of data retrieved with the [Analysis:startAnalysisQueries API](#/Analysis/startAnalysisQueries).
Example: If the column `type` is `string` as shown below, it can be treated as a string.
```json
{
  "name": "SIM_SESSION_EVENTS",
  "columnInfo": [
      {
          "name": "APN",
          "type": "string",
          "databaseType": "TEXT",

info.name: Search Soracom Inventory devices by query

params.name: name
params.description: Name to search.

params.name: group
params.description: Group name to search.

params.name: group_id
params.description: Group ID to search.

params.name: deviceId
params.description: Soracom Inventory device ID to search.

params.name: tag
params.description: String of tag values to search.

params.name: imsi
params.description: IMSI of the device that was used on bootstrapping.

params.name: imei
params.description: IMEI of the device that was used on bootstrapping.

params.name: limit
params.description: The maximum number of items to retrieve.

params.name: last_evaluated_key
params.description: The Soracom Inventory device ID of the last Inventory device retrieved on the previous page. By specifying this parameter, you can continue to retrieve the list from the next Inventory device onward.

params.name: search_type
params.description: Type of the search ('AND searching' or 'OR searching').

items.docs:
Search Soracom Inventory devices by query terms. It returns partial match results. When this API permission is allowed, it grants the authority to search and retrieve all Soracom Inventory devices that include their group information.

**Warning**: Use this API when the device ID of the target Inventory device is unknown, or when you want to retrieve a list of Inventory devices that match conditions. If you know the device ID, use the [Device:getDevice API](#/Device/getDevice).

params.name: name
params.description: Name to search.

params.name: group
params.description: Group name to search.

params.name: group_id
params.description: Group ID to search.

params.name: deviceId
params.description: Sigfox device ID to search.

params.name: tag
params.description: String of tag values to search.

params.name: status
params.description: Status of Sigfox devices.

params.name: registration
params.description: Registration status of Sigfox devices.

params.name: limit
params.description: The maximum number of items to retrieve.

params.name: last_evaluated_key
params.description: The Sigfox device ID of the last Sigfox device retrieved on the previous page. By specifying this parameter, you can continue to retrieve the list from the next Sigfox device onward.

params.name: search_type
params.description: Type of the search ('AND searching' or 'OR searching').

items.docs:
Search Sigfox devices by query terms. It returns partial match results. When this API permission is allowed, it grants the authority to search and retrieve all Sigfox devices that includes their group information.

**Warning**: Use this API when the device ID of the target Sigfox device is unknown, or when you want to retrieve a list of Sigfox devices that match conditions. If you know the device ID, use the [SigfoxDevice:getSigfoxDevice API](#/SigfoxDevice/getSigfoxDevice).

params.name: name
params.description: Name to search.

params.name: group
params.description: Name of the [group](/en/docs/groups/) to which the IoT SIM belongs.

params.name: group_id
params.description: Search for IoT SIMs whose group ID matches the specified value.

params.name: sim_id
params.description: Identifier of the SIM to search.

params.name: imsi
params.description: IMSI to search.

params.name: msisdn
params.description: MSISDN to search.

params.name: iccid
params.description: ICCID to search. An identifier used to identify a SIM card or virtual IoT SIM (Virtual SIM/Subscriber).

params.name: serial_number
params.description: Serial number to search. This is set only for IoT SIMs for specific regions.

params.name: tag
params.description: String of tag values to search. For more information, please refer to [Using Tags with Soracom Air](/docs/air/tags).

params.name: bundles
params.description: Bundles type to search.

params.name: status
params.description:
Status of the IoT SIM to search.

- `ready`
- `active`
- `inactive`
- `standby`
- `suspended`
- `terminated`
- `shipped`

params.name: session_status
params.description:
Status of the session to search. Specify one of the following:

- `NA`: Any.
- `ONLINE`: Online.
- `OFFLINE`: Offline.

params.name: subscription
params.description:
Subscription to search. Use exact match for the search. If specifying multiple subscriptions, please set `search_type` to `OR`.

- For Japan coverage, specify one of the following:
  - `plan-D`: plan-D (without bundle), plan-D (D-300MB).
  - `plan-K2`: plan-K2 (K2-300MB).
  - `plan-DU`
  - `plan-KM1`
  - `plan-K`
  - `planArc01`: Virtual SIM/Subscriber.
- For global coverage, specify one of the following:
  - `plan01s`
  - `plan01s-low_data_volume`: plan01s - Low Data Volume.
  - `planX3`: planX3 (X3-5MB), planX3.
  - `planP1`
  - `plan-US`
  - `plan-US-max`
  - `planX1`
  - `planX2`
  - `planX3-EU`
  - `plan-US-NA`
  - `planArc01`: Virtual SIM/Subscriber.

params.name: module_type
params.description:
The form factor of the physical SIM to search.

- `mini`: standard (2FF) size.
- `micro`: micro (3FF) size.
- `nano`: nano (4FF) size.
- `trio`: 3 in 1 (can be cut into 2FF/3FF/4FF depending on how you cut it).
- `embedded`: Embedded (MFF2).
- `virtual`: Virtual SIM/Subscriber.
- `integrated`: Embedded (iSIM).
- `profilePackage`: Profile Package (eSIM profile).

params.name: limit
params.description: The maximum number of items to retrieve.

params.name: last_evaluated_key
params.description: The SIM ID of the last SIM retrieved on the previous page. By specifying this parameter, you can continue to retrieve the list from the next SIM onward.

params.name: search_type
params.description:
The type of search condition.

- AND: SIMs which match all of the search parameters will be returned (default).
- OR: SIMs which match any of the search parameters will be returned.

If the value of a search parameter contains a comma `,` (or `%2C` when URL-encoded), the value will be split at each comma and treated as multiple search values, each of which will be evaluated based on the specified AND or OR condition.

items.docs:
Searches for SIMs using specified query parameters.

- Supports partial matching.
- Case-insensitive.
- Multiple search values can be specified for the following parameters by separating each value with a comma `,` (or `%2C` when URL-encoded). Note that the literal character `,` itself cannot be used as part of a search value.
    - `name`
    - `group`
    - `sim_id`
    - `imsi`
    - `msisdn`
    - `iccid`
    - `serial_number`
    - `tag`
    - `status`
    - `subscription`
    - `module_typ

items.docs: (DEPRECATED: please consider to use `/query/sims` API instead ) Search subscribers by query terms. It returns partial match results. When this API permission is allowed, it grants the authority to search and retrieve all SIMs that includes their group information.

params.description: The end point of searching range (UNIX time in milliseconds).

params.name: order
params.description: The order of ranking.

The type of the resource summary.

- `simsPerStatus`: The number of IoT SIMs per status.
